text:
Exchange × Revenge × Judgment Chain (交渉×復讐×律する鎖, Kōshō × Fukushū × Rissuru Kusari) is the 69 th episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime and the 7th episode of the Yorknew City OVA series. It was released on DVD on April 17 th , 2002.

Overview

Pakunoda meets with Kurapika and agrees to his conditions for the hostage exchange. Illumi switches places with Hisoka so he can go after Chrollo.

Synopsis

At the Phantom Troupe's hideout, Hisoka, Franklin, and Bonolenov wait for the other Troupe members to return. They hear a suspicious noise nearby, and Franklin suggests they check it out together. However, Hisoka goes off on his own, claiming he will be fine. Franklin and Bonolenov spot a child escaping quickly from the hideout. They debate going after the child, but worry it might be a trap, so they decide not to. Illumi then enters the hideout disguised as Hisoka and rejoins the two other Troupe members.

Outside the Lingon Airport, Pakunoda gets a phone call from Kurapika, who instructs her on which airship to board. She does as told, and after boarding the airship, it takes off. Kurapika, along with Melody and a chained up Chrollo, meet with her. Kurapika gives Pakunoda two conditions to accept in exchange for releasing Chrollo, the first being that Chrollo must never use his Nen again. Kurapika takes a long time to consider the second condition, especially since he worries greatly about potentially losing Gon and Killua if he messes this exchange up; he ends up deciding that the second condition will be that Chrollo must cease all contact with the other Spiders. Pakunoda agrees to the conditions on Chrollo's behalf, and Kurapika makes the contract official with his Judgment Chain. For Pakunoda herself, he has her agree to release Gon and Killua at midnight and not to spread any information about Kurapika, which she also agrees to.

During this time, Melody observes that Kurapika's heartbeat is not calm like it normally is and that he appears very hostile towards Pakunoda and unable to think objectively, worrying about him and what he might do. Even though Melody sympathizes with what Kurapika has gone through, she also doesn't believe Pakunoda to be a completely evil person, considering her willingness to help her boss and give back to Kurapika's friends. Kurapika has Pakunoda leave, instructing her to return at midnight with Gon and Killua and without any other Troupe members. However, before Pakunoda can leave, Kurapika lashes out at her for not asking more questions or seeming at all worried about this potentially being a trap, but Pakunoda is unfazed by him.

At the Troupe's hideout, Pakunoda refuses to tell the others of Kurapika's location or allow them to come with her, despite Phinks' insistence that they should just kill Gon and Killua and then Kurapika. Pakunoda seems ready to take on Phinks and the Troupe members on his side, before Machi steps between them and tells Pakunoda to go. Machi and Kortopi stand in Phinks and Feitan Portor's way, ready to oppose them. Gon, unable to keep quiet after watching this dispute, yells at Phinks and Feitan for not understanding why the others would want to save their comrade. Gon also vouches for Kurapika, saying that he wouldn't kill Pakunoda or Chrollo if he had promised not to, no matter how angry he was, and that they can trust him to do the exchange fairly. Phinks threatens Gon for speaking up, but Franklin interrupts to calm the group down and says that they should let Pakunoda go, and rebuild the Spider if things go wrong. Phinks gets a phone call from Kurapika, who asks to speak with the hostages. Kurapika checks up on Gon and confirms with him that all of the Spiders are at the hideout as agreed. Phinks then confirms to Kurapika that Pakunoda will bring the hostages to him alone.

While she walks with them, Pakunoda questions why Gon and Killua don't just run away from her, since they could easily get away at the moment. They claim that it is much easier for them to go through the hostage exchange, since Kurapika is their friend and they don't want to see him kill anyone, and Pakunoda is taken aback by their response. At the airport, Leorio, Kurapika, and Melody watch from their airship as Pakunoda, Gon, and Killua arrive. However, Leorio also spots someone else approaching, and they worry it might be a trap. At that moment, Kurapika's cell phone rings.
